skill.name: agent-ui
skill.description: Agent UI helps teams add a full React or Next.js agent interface using the ui.inference.sh component. It covers setup, proxy routing, streaming responses, human approval flows, client-side tools, widgets, and file or image options.
skill.summary: Build React and Next.js agent interfaces with streaming, tools, approvals, and widgets.
skill.author: inference-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network URLs, environment access, weak cryptography, and a dangerous-combination heuristic. Review found the command, URL, and environment patterns are documentation examples, not executable skill code, but the quick start does direct users to install a remote component with npx. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or confirmed malicious behavior was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Remote Component Installation Command
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The quick start instructs users to run npx shadcn@latest add with a remote ui.inference.sh component URL. This is a legitimate setup pattern for a UI component skill, but users should review the fetched component before installing third-party code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is shown directly in the quick start and retrieves component code from a network URL. The surrounding context is installation documentation, so the risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: API Key Environment Setup Guidance
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The skill tells users to place an INFERENCE_API_KEY value in .env.local. This is expected configuration guidance, but it involves a secret and should be handled with normal environment-file protections.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows an example API key environment variable, but no code reads or transmits it in the skill content. The concern is secret-handling guidance, not observed exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links to External Sites
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to ui.inference.sh and inference.sh documentation, component, and article pages. They are relevant references for the skill and do not show hidden data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links in sections labeled Quick Start and Documentation. No evidence found that they are used for covert network requests.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Mostly Fenced Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The repeated command-execution findings are caused by Markdown code fences and inline property names. SKILL.md contains instructions and examples, not runnable automation inside the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are Markdown examples for Next.js, React, props, and related skill installation. No executable wrapper, script file, or hidden command launcher is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static analysis combined command examples, URLs, and an API key placeholder into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no credential exfiltration, obfuscation, prompt injection, or malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The individual signals are visible documentation content with a coherent agent UI setup purpose. No evidence found for malicious behavior beyond supply-chain risk from the documented remote install command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic operations. They appear to match unrelated words, headings, or component references in the Markdown documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, checksum, or cryptographic API usage at the reported lines. The finding lacks semantic support in the only scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Based Package and Component Retrieval
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start uses npx and a remote component registry URL. This pattern can install third-party code and should be reviewed by users before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented and involves remote code retrieval. It is common for shadcn workflows, but it still creates a supply-chain review requirement.

content.user_title: Build Agent UIs for React and Next.js
content.value_statement: Teams need agent interfaces that handle streaming, tools, approvals, and rich responses without rebuilding every UI pattern. This skill shows how to add the ui.inference.sh Agent component to a React or Next.js app.
actual_capabilities: Provides quick-start commands for installing the Agent component and SDK.
Shows a Next.js proxy route using the inferencesh SDK proxy helper.
Documents configuration for model references, system prompts, and proxy URLs.
Explains built-in support for streaming, approvals, widgets, and tool lifecycle states.
Includes an example for client-side tools scoped to a form element.
Lists optional props for file uploads, image uploads, naming, and agent configuration.
limitations: Requires a React or Next.js project that can use the documented component pattern.
Requires an inference.sh API key and related service access for runtime use.
Does not include backend business logic beyond the documented proxy route.
Remote component installation should be reviewed before use in production.

use_cases.title: Add a SaaS Copilot
use_cases.description: Add an embedded assistant that streams responses, calls tools, and asks for approval before sensitive actions.

use_cases.title: Prototype Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Create a working agent interface quickly while testing prompts, model references, widgets, and tool behavior.

use_cases.title: Build Form-Filling Assistants
use_cases.description: Connect client-side tools to page elements so an agent can inspect and fill supported form fields.

prompt_templates.title: Install the Agent Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup in an existing Next.js project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-ui skill to guide me through adding the Agent component and SDK to my Next.js app.

prompt_templates.title: Create the Proxy Route
prompt_templates.scenario: Backend route setup for inference traffic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-ui skill to add the documented inference proxy route and explain where my API key should live.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Tools and Approvals
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-ui skill to configure an Agent component with client-side tools, approval states, and streaming enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the agent-ui skill to review my Agent component integration for proxy routing, secret handling, file options, and tool scope.

output_examples.input: Add an agent assistant to a Next.js dashboard.
output_examples.output: A setup plan covering component installation, SDK dependency, proxy route placement, API key configuration, and Agent component usage.

output_examples.input: Create a form assistant that can fill fields after user approval.
output_examples.output: A component integration outline that connects scoped client tools to a form and describes expected approval and progress states.

output_examples.input: Check whether my current agent UI setup is production ready.
output_examples.output: A review checklist covering remote component review, proxy URL configuration, model reference selection, upload settings, and secret storage.

best_practices: Review remote component code before running installation commands in a production repository.
Store API keys in environment files or secret managers that are excluded from version control.
Keep client-side tools narrowly scoped to the UI elements they are allowed to inspect or modify.
anti_patterns: Do not commit .env.local or real inference API keys to a repository.
Do not enable file or image uploads unless the product needs them and handles the data safely.
Do not give browser tools broad page access when a form-scoped tool is sufficient.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps add an Agent component to React or Next.js apps with streaming, tools, approvals, and widgets.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented setup uses an INFERENCE_API_KEY value for the inference proxy route.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill include backend application logic?
faq.answer: No. It documents a proxy route and component setup, but product-specific backend logic remains your responsibility.

faq.question: Why is the security risk marked medium?
faq.answer: The skill documents a remote npx component installation and API key setup, which require review and careful secret handling.

faq.question: Are the static critical findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found documentation examples and links, not confirmed malicious behavior or prompt injection.
